Title: CRISPR/Cas9-based genome editing of 14 lipid metabolic genes reveals a sporopollenin metabolon ZmPKSB-ZmTKPR1-1/-2 required for pollen exine formation in maize

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces tetraketide α-pyrone produced by ZmPKSB to reduced tetraketide α-pyrone using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forms pollen exine required for pollen wall integrity and male fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S (experimental expression constructs); native promoter assayed in dual-luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step in sporopollenin precursor formation within the metabolon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 single and double knockout; cytology (SEM/TEM/transverse sections); subcellular localization; enzyme assay with recombinant protein; site-directed mutagenesis; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pollen exine formation and contributes to anther cuticle formation; loss together with ZmTKPR1-2 causes complete male sterility, delayed tapetal degeneration, absent Ubisch bodies, very thin/disorganized exine, and smooth anther cuticle.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmTKPR1-1 encodes a tetraketide α-pyrone reductase required redundantly with ZmTKPR1-2 for maize male fertility. 2. It reduces ZmPKSB-derived tetraketide α-pyrone to reduced tetraketide α-pyrone, a sporopollenin precursor. 3. Conserved residues S128, Y164 and K168 are essential for catalytic activity. 4. It physically interacts with ZmPKSB and ZmTKPR1-2 in a sporopollenin metabolon. 5. It is directly activated by ZmMYB84.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutants showed only slight partial male sterility, indicating redundancy with ZmTKPR1-2. ZmTKPR1-1 showed greater reductase activity than ZmTKPR1-2, and combined presence of both increased catalytic activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes complete male sterility in double mutant and defective anther cuticle.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Anther RNA-seq in B73, Zheng58 and M6007 (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering genic male sterility and male-sterility line-assisted maize breeding.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces tetraketide α-pyrone produced by ZmPKSB to reduced tetraketide α-pyrone using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forms pollen exine required for pollen wall integrity and male fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S (experimental expression constructs); native promoter assayed in dual-luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step in sporopollenin precursor formation within the metabolon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 single and double knockout; cytology (SEM/TEM/transverse sections); subcellular localization; enzyme assay with recombinant protein; site-directed mutagenesis; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pollen exine formation and contributes to anther cuticle formation; loss together with ZmTKPR1-1 causes complete male sterility, delayed tapetal degeneration, absent Ubisch bodies, very thin/disorganized exine, and smooth anther cuticle.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmTKPR1-2 encodes a tetraketide α-pyrone reductase required redundantly with ZmTKPR1-1 for maize male fertility. 2. It reduces ZmPKSB-derived tetraketide α-pyrone to reduced tetraketide α-pyrone, a sporopollenin precursor. 3. Conserved residues S130, Y166 and K170 are essential for catalytic activity. 4. It physically interacts with ZmPKSB and ZmTKPR1-1 in a sporopollenin metabolon. 5. It is directly activated by ZmMYB84.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutants showed only slight partial male sterility, indicating redundancy with ZmTKPR1-1. ZmTKPR1-2 had lower reductase activity than ZmTKPR1-1, but both together increased catalytic activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes complete male sterility in double mutant and defective anther cuticle.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Anther RNA-seq in B73, Zheng58 and M6007 (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering genic male sterility and male-sterility line-assisted maize breeding.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA with C16:0-CoA or C18:0-CoA to generate tri- and tetraketide α-pyrone intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forms pollen exine required for pollen wall integrity and male fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: similar anther developmental expression pattern to ZmTKPR1-1/-2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: similar anther developmental expression pattern to ZmTKPR1-1/-2
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: upstream key step in sporopollenin precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping cited in this paper; qPCR; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; AlphaFold2 docking; recombinant enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal pollen exine formation and affects anther cuticle; mutation causes male sterility, thinner exine, and denser anther cuticle relative to WT.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPKSB acts upstream of ZmTKPR1-1/-2 to generate tetraketide α-pyrone substrates. 2. It physically interacts with ZmTKPR1-1 and ZmTKPR1-2 to form a sporopollenin metabolon. 3. In combination with ZmTKPR1-1/-2, it supports sequential production of reduced tetraketide α-pyrone. 4. pksb mutation reduces ZmTKPR1-1/-2 transcript levels and causes distinct cuticle/exine phenotypes from tkpr1-1/-2.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper relies partly on previous work for ZmPKSB function. pksb and tkpr1-1/-2 show opposite anther cuticle phenotypes despite belonging to the same metabolon.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes male sterility and altered cutin/wax balance.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Anther RNA-seq in B73, Zheng58 and M6007 (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Component of a male-sterility module useful for hybrid breeding and metabolic engineering of reproductive lipid pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forms pollen exine required for pollen wall integrity and male fertility.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sporopollenin precursor biosynthesis and associated anther cuticle/pollen exine formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates ZmTKPR1-1 and ZmTKPR1-2 promoters; expression of ZmTKPR1-1/-2 is almost undetectable in myb84 anthers; previous work showed ZmPKSB expression depends on ZmMYB84.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct upstream activator of multiple sporopollenin metabolon genes required for exine formation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: overlaps with ZmTKPR1-1/-2 from S8a to S9-10; peaks at S9
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: overlaps with ZmTKPR1-1/-2 from S8a to S9-10; peaks at S9
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-Ω effector in transient assay; native ZmTKPR1-1 and ZmTKPR1-2 promoters in reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in myb84 anthers; transient dual-luciferase reporter assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates sporopollenin metabolon genes and promotes pollen exine formation and male fertility.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB84 is necessary for ZmTKPR1-1 and ZmTKPR1-2 expression in anthers. 2. It directly binds their promoters and activates transcription. 3. Together with prior evidence for ZmPKSB, ZmMYB84 acts as an upstream regulator of the ZmPKSB-ZmTKPR1 metabolon.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study cites previous work showing ZmMYB84 is required for tapetal PCD and pollen exine formation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss likely causes male sterility through disrupted exine formation, as referenced from prior work.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR-supported; anther RNA-seq context provided without accession
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream switch for controlling male sterility and exine-related reproductive lipid metabolism in maize.
